definition: A thing whose name cannot be remembered; thingamajig, doohickey.
A device to restrain speech, such as a rag in the mouth secured with tape or a rubber ball threaded onto a cord or strap.
The Milky Way; the apparent band of concentrated stars which appears in the night sky over earth.
Brave, valiant, courteous, especially with regard to male attitudes towards women.
An institution, building, or room for the exhibition and conservation of important objects, especially works of art.
A long, slender ship propelled primarily by oars, whether having masts and sails or not; usually a rowed warship used in the Mediterranean from the 16th century until the modern era.
A unit of volume, equivalent to eight pints
The fastest gait of a horse, a two-beat stride during which all four legs are off the ground simultaneously.
To coat with a layer of metal by electrochemical means.
A census-designated place in Apache County, Arizona, United States.
A member of a criminal or street gang.
An opening in anything made by breaking or parting.
To open the mouth wide, especially involuntarily, as in a yawn, anger, or surprise.
A building (or section of a building) used to store a car or cars, tools and other miscellaneous items.
Food waste material of any kind.
An outdoor area containing one or more types of plants, usually plants grown for food or ornamental purposes.
One who gardens; one who grows plants or cultivates a garden.
The process or action of cultivating the soil, particularly in a garden; the care of a garden; horticulture.
Coarse wool on the legs of sheep.
To clean one's mouth by holding water or some other liquid in the back of the mouth and blowing air out from the lungs.
Overly ostentatious; so colorful as to be in bad taste.
A circular or linear decoration, especially one of plaited flowers or leaves, worn on the body or draped as a decoration.
A plant, Allium sativum, related to the onion, having a pungent bulb much used in cooking.
A granary; a store of grain.
To decorate with ornaments; to adorn; to embellish.
A male waiter (especially at a French restaurant).

Matter in an intermediate state between liquid and plasma that can be contained only if it is fully surrounded by a solid (or in a bubble of liquid, or held together by gravitational pull); it can condense into a liquid, or can (rarely) become a solid directly by deposition.
A flammable liquid consisting of a mixture of refined petroleum hydrocarbons, mainly used as a motor fuel; petrol.
To draw in the breath suddenly, as if from a shock.
Of or relating to the stomach.
A kind of pastry in Armenia and some neighboring countries.
A doorlike structure outside a house.
A passage that can be closed by use of a gate.
A meeting or get-together; a party or social function.
Awkward or lacking in social graces; bumbling; apt to make gaffes.
A measure; a standard of measure; an instrument to determine dimensions, distance, or capacity; a standard
Protective armor for the hands, formerly thrown down as a challenge to combat.
An English surname transferred from the nickname, originally a nickname for a cheerful or lively person.
A nickname of the given name Gareth or Gary.
Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
A precious stone, usually of substantial monetary value or prized for its beauty or shine.
A theoretical unit of heredity of living organisms which may take several values and (in principle) predetermines a precise trait of an organism's form (phenotype), such as hair color.
The descent of a person, family, or group from an ancestor or ancestors; lineage or pedigree.
Including or involving every part or member of a given or implied entity, whole, etc.; common to all, universal.
An act or instance of generalizing; concluding that something true of a subclass is true of the entire class.
To speak in generalities, or in vague terms.
To bring into being; give rise to.
The act of creating something or bringing something into being; production, creation.
One who, or that which, generates, begets, causes, or produces.
Very broad; pertaining or appropriate to large classes or groups as opposed to specific instances.
The trait of being willing to donate money, resources, or time.
Noble in behaviour or actions; principled, not petty; kind, magnanimous.
In a generous manner, in a way that is giving or ample.
The origin, start, or point at which something comes into being.
The branch of biology that deals with the transmission and variation of inherited characteristics, in particular chromosomes and DNA.
Of or relating to biological reproduction.
Someone possessing extraordinary intelligence or skill; especially somebody who has demonstrated this by a creative or original work in science, music, art, etc.
A kind; a stylistic category or sort, especially of literature or other artworks.

A legally defined unit of Roman society, being a collection of people related through a common ancestor by birth, marriage or adoption, possibly over many generations, and sharing the same nomen gentilicium.
Affectedly proper or refined; somewhat prudish refinement; excessively polite.
Tender and amiable; of a considerate or kindly disposition.
Any well-bred, well-mannered, or charming man.
In a manner characterized by gentleness
Belonging to, or proceeding from the original stock; native
In a genuine manner; truthfully, truly.
A category in the classification of organisms, ranking below family (Lat. familia) and above species.
The study of the physical properties of the earth, including how humans affect and are affected by them.
The science that studies the structure of the Earth (or other planets), together with its origin and development, especially by examination of its rocks.
The branch of mathematics dealing with spatial relationships.
The small mass of cells from which a new organism develops; a seed, bud, spore, or zygote.
An ancient Roman term for a cultural region describing the lands in Central Europe inhabited by Germanic peoples.
A nation or civilization occupying the country around the Rhine, Elbe, and upper Danube Rivers in Central Europe, taken as a whole under its various governments.
Of a seed, to begin to grow, to sprout roots and leaves.
A motion of the limbs or body, especially one made to emphasize speech.
Said to someone who has just sneezed.
third-person singular simple present indicative of get
present participle and gerund of get
Like a ghost in appearance; death-like; pale; pallid; dismal.
An (often walled) area of a city in which Jews are concentrated by force and law. (Used particularly of areas in medieval Italy and in Nazi-controlled Europe.)
A disembodied soul; a soul or spirit of a deceased person; a spirit appearing after death.
Of or pertaining to ghosts or spirits.
A mythical human or humanoid of very great size.
Feeling a sense of spinning in the head, causing a perception of unsteadiness and being about to fall down; dizzy.
Something given to another voluntarily, without charge.
Endowed with special, in particular intellectual, abilities.
Originally (music), a performing engagement by a musician or musical group; (by extension, film, television, theater) a job or role for a performer.
To laugh gently in a playful, nervous, or affected manner.
To cover with a thin layer of gold; to cover with gold leaf.
A breathing organ of fish and other aquatic animals.
Gold or other metal in a thin layer; gilding.
A colourless non-aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries; the base for many cocktails.
The pungent aromatic rhizome of a tropical Asian herb, Zingiber officinale, used as a spice and as a stimulant and acarminative.
In a cautious and delicate manner; (very) carefully or cautiously.

A ruminant in the genus Giraffa of the African savannah with long legs, a highly elongated neck, yellow fur patterned with dark spots typically in the form of a network, and two or more horn-like projections called ossicones; the tallest living animal.
To bind with a flexible rope or cord.
A female partner in an unmarried romantic relationship.
To move, shift, provide something abstract or concrete to someone or something or somewhere.
third-person singular simple present indicative of give
present participle and gerund of give
A large body of ice which flows under its own mass, usually downhill.

Originally, enchantment; magic charm; especially, the effect of a spell that causes one to see objects in a form that differs from reality, typically to make filthy, ugly, or repulsive things seem beauteous.
To turn (one's eyes or look) at something, often briefly.

A specialized cell, group of cells, or organ of endothelial origin in the human or animal body that synthesizes a chemical substance, such as hormones or breast milk, and releases it, often into the bloodstream (endocrine gland) or into cavities inside the body or its outer surface (exocrine gland).
An amorphous solid, often transparent substance, usually made by melting silica sand with various additives (for most purposes, a mixture of soda, potash and lime is added).
The vitreous coating of pottery or porcelain; anything used as a coating or color in glazing.
An appearance of light, especially one which is indistinct or small, or short-lived.
To collect (fruit, grain, or other produce) from a field, an orchard, etc., after the main gathering or harvest.
Joy; happiness; great delight, especially from one's own good fortune or from another's misfortune.
Having a ready flow of words but lacking thought or understanding; superficial; shallow.
To move softly, smoothly, or effortlessly.
A faint light; a dim glow.
To see or view (someone, or something tangible) briefly and incompletely.
A short flash of light, usually when reflected off a shiny surface.
To reflect light with a glittering luster; to sparkle, coruscate, glint or flash.
A bright, sparkling light; shininess or brilliance.
To exhibit a conspicuous (sometimes malevolent) pleasure or sense of self-satisfaction, often at an adversary's misfortune.
Concerning all parts of the world.
Not very illuminated; dim because of darkness, especially when appearing depressing or frightening.
A lightweight fabric used for umbrellas and dresses.
Exhibiting attributes, qualities, or acts that are worthy of or receive glory.
A list of difficult words or specialized terms used in a particular book or document, or in a particular domain of knowledge, with their definitions; a list of glosses (explanatory annotations).
Having a smooth, silklike, reflective (shiny) surface.
An item of clothing, covering all or part of the hand and fingers, but usually allowing independent movement of the fingers.
To emit heat and light without a flame.
present participle and gerund of glow
A simple monosaccharide (sugar) with a molecular formula of C₆H₁₂O₆; a principle source of energy for cellular metabolism.
A hard gelatin made by boiling bones and hides, used in solution as an adhesive; or any sticky adhesive substance.
To bite something persistently, especially something tough.
Abbreviation of Gorontalo: a province of Indonesia.
A long, pointed stick used to prod animals.
A result that one is attempting to achieve.
A designated player that attempts to prevent the opposing team from scoring by protecting a goal.
Any ruminant of the genus Capra.
Traditionally, a supernatural being of folklore, typically small and grotesque or misshapen, that commonly haunts dark places, often mischievous or malevolent; a type of evil elf, sprite, or demon.
A deity or supreme being; a supernatural, typically immortal, being with superior powers, to which personhood is attributed.
A man present at the christening of a baby who promises to help raise the child in a Christian manner; a male godparent who sponsors the baptism of a child.
A woman present at the christening of a baby who promises to help raise the child in a Christian manner; a female godparent who sponsors the baptism of a child.
third-person singular simple present indicative of go
present participle and gerund of go
A heavy yellow elemental metal of great value, with atomic number 79 and symbol Au.
Made of, or relating to, gold.
A type of small fish, Carassius auratus, typically orange-colored.
A person who makes, repairs or sells things of gold, especially jewelry.
A ball game played by individuals competing against one another in which the object is to hit a ball into each of a series of (usually 18 or nine) holes in the minimum number of strokes.
Of a person or an animal:
Farewell; a formula used to another person or persons when the speaker, writer, or person addressed is departing.
The state or characteristic of being good.
A favorably disposed attitude toward someone or something.
Used to indicate pleasure or delight.
Any of various grazing waterfowl of the family Anatidae, which have feathers and webbed feet and are capable of flying, swimming, and walking on land, and which are generally bigger than ducks.
The front aspect of the neck; the outside of the throat.
An ape of the genus Gorilla; endemic to the forests of Central Africa and typified by superlative physical size and strength in relation to other primates.
The first section of the Christian New Testament scripture, comprising the books of Matthew, Mark, Luke and John, concerned with the birth, ministry, passion, and resurrection of Jesus.
Someone who likes to talk about other people's private or personal business.
To make and administer the public policy and affairs of; to exercise sovereign authority in.
present participle and gerund of govern
The body with the power to make and/or enforce laws to control a country, land area, people or organization.
The chief executive officer of a first-level administrative division of a country.
A light meal in the afternoon; tea.
To grip suddenly; to seize; to clutch.
Having or showing grace in movement, shape, or proportion.
A machine used in road maintenance, construction, and mining for leveling large surfaces.
A small town in Gorizia, Friuli-Venezia Giulia.
Proceeding or advancing by small, slow, regular steps or degrees
In a gradual manner; making slow progress; slowly.
A person who is recognized by a university as having completed the requirements of a degree studied at the institution.
The action or process of graduating and receiving a diploma for completing a course of study (such as from an educational institution).
Drawings or words drawn on a surface in a public place, usually made without authorization.
A small shoot or scion of a tree inserted in another tree, the stock of which is to support and nourish it. The two unite and become one tree, but the graft determines the kind of fruit.
The harvested seeds of various grass food crops eg: wheat, corn, barley.
A unit of mass equal to one-thousandth of a kilogram. Symbol: g.
Various species of grass in the genus Bouteloua, including Bouteloua gracilis (blue grama)
A system of rules and principles for the structure of a language, or of languages in general.
Not breaching any constraints of the grammar, or morpho-syntax, of the relevant language.
Large, senior (high-ranking), intense, extreme, or exceptional
Of a cup of coffee: smaller than venti but larger than tall, usually 16 ounces (~ 455 ml).
The state of being grand or splendid; magnificence.
Large and impressive, in size, scope or extent.

A group of igneous and plutonic rocks composed primarily of feldspar and quartz. Usually contains one or more dark minerals, which may be mica, pyroxene, or amphibole. Granite is quarried for building stone, road gravel, decorative stone, and tombstones. Common colors are gray, white, pink, and yellow-brown.
A small, round, smooth-skinned edible fruit, usually purple, red, or green, that grows in bunches on female vines of most species of genus Vitis and the self-fertile plants of species Vitis vinifera.
The tree of the species Citrus paradisi, a hybrid of Citrus maxima and sweet orange.

A data chart (graphical representation of data) intended to illustrate the relationship between a set (or sets) of numbers (quantities, measurements or indicative numbers) and a reference set, whose elements are indexed to those of the former set(s) and may or may not be numbers.

An allotrope of carbon, consisting of planes of carbon atoms arranged in hexagonal arrays with the planes stacked loosely, that is used as a dry lubricant, in "lead" pencils, and as a moderator in some nuclear reactors.
To seize something and hold it firmly.
To grip; to take hold, particularly with the hand.
Any plant of the family Poaceae, characterized by leaves that arise from nodes in the stem and leaf bases that wrap around the stem, especially those grown as ground cover rather than for grain.

A mostly herbivorous insect of the order Orthoptera, noted for its ability to jump long distances and for the habit of some species communicating by stridulation; they are related to but distinct from crickets.
A horizontal metal grill through which liquid, ash, or small objects can fall, while larger objects cannot.
Of a person or their actions, feelings, etc.: expressing gratitude or appreciation; appreciative, thankful.
The act of gratifying or pleasing, either the mind, or the appetite or taste.
An additional payment given freely as thanks for service.
An excavation in the earth as a place of burial.
Small fragments of rock, used for laying on the beds of roads and railways, and as ballast.
A stone slab set at the head of a grave.
A tract of land in which the dead are buried.

The fundamental force of attraction which exists between all matter in the universe that tends to draw bodies towards each other, due to matter causing the curvature of spacetime. It is the weakest of the four fundamental interactions, and possesses a gauge boson known as the graviton.
A thick sauce made from the fat or juices that come out from meat or vegetables as they are being cooked.
Of a color between black and white, having neutral hue and intermediate brightness.
The act of grazing; a scratching or injuring lightly on passing.
Animal fat in a melted or soft state.
Having a slippery surface; having a surface covered with grease.
To a great extent or degree.
The state, condition, or quality of being great
A selfish or excessive desire for more than is needed or deserved, especially of money, wealth, food, or other possessions.
Having greed; consumed by selfish desires.
The color of grass and leaves; a primary additive color midway between yellow and blue which is evoked by light between roughly 495–570 nm.
A building used to grow plants, particularly one with large glass windows or plastic sheeting to trap heat from sunlight even in intemperate seasons or climates.
To welcome in a friendly manner, either in person or through another means such as writing.
A conventional phrase used to start a letter or conversation or otherwise to acknowledge a person's arrival or presence.
Who enjoys being in crowds and socializing.
A small explosive device, designed to be thrown by hand or launched using a rifle, grenade launcher, or rocket.
A lean breed of dog used in hunting and racing.
A rectangular array of squares or rectangles of equal size, such as in a crossword puzzle.
To cause sorrow or distress to.

A grating; a grid of wire or a sheet of material with a pattern of holes or slots, usually used to protect something while allowing the passage of air and liquids. Typical uses: to allow air through a fan while preventing fingers or objects from passing; to allow people to talk to somebody, while preventing attack.
One who, or that which, grills.
Dismal and gloomy, cold and forbidding.
A contorted facial expression, often expressing contempt or pain.
A smile in which the lips are parted to reveal the teeth.
To reduce to smaller pieces by crushing with lateral motion.
To take hold (of), particularly with the hand.
A step (in a flight of stairs); a degree.
A collection of hard small materials, such as dirt, ground stone, debris from sandblasting or other such grinding, or swarf from metalworking.
A low, mournful sound uttered in pain or grief.
A person who retails groceries (foodstuffs and household items) from a grocery.
Retail foodstuffs and other household supplies.
The crease or depression of the human body at the junction of the trunk and the thigh, together with the surrounding region.
A man who is about to marry.
A long, narrow channel or depression; e.g., such a slot cut into a hard material to provide a location for an engineering component, a tire groove, or a geological channel or depression.
To feel with or use the hands; to handle.
third-person singular simple present indicative of gross
Distorted and unnatural in shape or size; abnormal, especially in a hideous way.
The surface of the Earth, as opposed to the sky or water or underground.
Without any grounds to support it; baseless.
The foundation; the basic or fundamental parts that support or allow for the rest.
A number of things or persons being in some relation to one another.
present participle and gerund of group

Any of various game birds of the subfamily Tetraoninae which inhabit temperate and subarctic regions of the Northern Hemisphere; specifically, the red grouse (Lagopus lagopus scotica) native to heather moorland on the British Isles.
To become larger, to increase in magnitude.
A farmer; one who grows things.
A deep, rumbling, threatening sound made in the throat by an animal.
An increase in size, number, value, or strength.
Deep-seated and/or long-term animosity or ill will about something or someone, especially due to perceived mistreatment.
Repellently frightful and shocking; ghastly, horrific.
A high thundering, rumbling or growling sound.
A short snorting sound, often to show disapproval, or used as a reply when one is reluctant to speak.
Anything that assures a certain outcome.
A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.
simple past and past participle of guard
A surname from the Romance languages.
Someone who guards, watches over, or protects.
A soldier in a small independent group, fighting against the government or regular forces by surprise raids.
To reach a partly (or totally) unconfirmed conclusion; to engage in conjecture; to speculate.
A recipient of hospitality, especially someone staying by invitation at the house of another.
The act or process of guiding.
Someone who guides, especially someone hired to show people around a place or an institution and offer information and explanation, or to lead them through dangerous terrain.
A non-specific rule or principle that provides direction to action or behaviour.

A group or association mainly of tradespeople made up of merchants, craftspeople, or artisans, particularly in the Middle Ages, established to oversee and protect their specific commercial interests and to provide mutual aid.
A customary way of speaking or acting; a fashion, a manner, a practice (often used formerly in such phrases as "at his own guise"; that is, in his own fashion, to suit himself.)
A stringed musical instrument, of European origin, usually with a fretted fingerboard and six strings, played with the fingers or a plectrum (guitar pick).
A hollow place in the earth; an abyss; a deep chasm or basin.
A seabird of the genus Larus or of the subfamily Larinae.
Easily deceived or duped; naive, easily cheated or fooled.
A trench, ravine or narrow channel which was worn by water flow, especially on a hillside.
A device for shooting projectiles or ditto (a shooter), functioning through stored energy: a firearm, cannon, harpoon gun/spear gun, raygun, etc, not a bow and arrow, or slingshot.
A person armed with a gun, especially a gunned evildoer.
An explosive mixture of saltpetre (potassium nitrate), charcoal and sulfur; formerly used in gunnery but now mostly used in fireworks.
The act of discharging a firearm.
To flow with a bubbling sound.
In Indian traditions: a spiritual teacher who transmits knowledge to a shishya.
A strong, abrupt rush of wind.
The alimentary canal, especially the intestine.
A prepared channel in a surface, especially at the side of a road adjacent to a curb, intended for the drainage of water.
An effigy of a man burned on a bonfire on the anniversary of the Gunpowder Plot (5th November).
Clipping of gymnasium; especially, a school gymnasium.
A large room or building for indoor sports.
A sport involving the performance of sequences of movements requiring physical strength, flexibility, and kinesthetic awareness.
